<RAW Variable>)

The value of <RAW Variable>

map_definition: If MODULE is not missing then MODULE else ‘<RAW Dataset>’

description: Concatenates multiple character variables and/or strings
map_definition: Concatenate parts: <Variable1>, ‘<String1>’, <Variable2>, …

description: Creates the Sequence Number
map_definition:
Sequence Number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records within the domain. 
Dataset structure: <Dataset Layout>

description:
Assign variable based on condition

The <Condition> is SAS code expression
<When True> and <When False> is SAS code or Mapping Function
map_definition: If <Condition> then <When True> else <When False>

description: Creates the DTC from date variable
map_definition: The date value of <Date Variable> in ISO8601 format.

description: Creates the DTC from date and time variables
map_definition: The date/time value of <Date Variable> and a time variable <Time Variable> in ISO8601 format.

function_name: DY(<Reference Library>.<Reference Dataset>.<Reference Variable>, <DTC Library>.<DTC Dataset>.<DTC Variable>)
description: Calculates the days between reference variable and DTC variable
code: %dy(invar_refdtc=<Reference Library>.<Reference Dataset>.<Reference Variable>, invar_dtc=<DTC Library>.<DTC Dataset>.<DTC Variable>, outvar_dy=WORK.<SDTM Dataset>.<SDTM Variable>);
map_definition: Calculates the days between a reference date (<Reference Dataset>.<Reference Variable>) and a date (<DTC Dataset>.<DTC Variable>). If date is prior to reference date, then <SDTM Dataset>.<SDTM Variable> = <DTC Dataset>.<DTC Variable>-<Reference Dataset>.<Reference Variable>. Otherwise <SDTM Dataset>.<SDTM Variable> = <DTC Dataset>.<DTC Variable>-<Reference Dataset>.<Reference Variable> +1.

description: Assigns an Observation ID based on invar variables
code: %qsobsid( outvar_obsid=WORK.<SDTM Dataset>.<SDTM Variable>, invar_obsid1-6=S.<RAW Dataset>.<RAW Variable>);
map_definition: Assign <SDTM Variable> based on variables: <invar_obsid1-6 Variables comma delimited>.

description: Returns the highest value of the listed values
map_definition: The highest value of: <Variable1/Value1>, <Variable2/Value2>, …

description: Returns the lowest value of the listed values
map_definition: The lowest value of: <Variable1/Value1>, <Variable2/Value2>, …

description: Compress the <Expression>
map_definition: The compressed value of <Expression>

description: Adds the CDISC Notes to the mapping definition
map_definition: <CDISC Notes>

description: Join source variable to target using a left join (SQL)
code:
%addvars(invar_1=<Source Library.Dataset.Variable>, inform_1=<Source Variable>, outvar_1=<WORK.Target Dataset.Source Variable>, invar_join_l1=<WORK.Target Dataset.Join Variable>, invar_join_r1=<Source Library.Source Dataset.Join Variable>, …)

Example:
%addvars(invar_1=SDTM.TV.VISITDY,  outvar_1=WORK.CE.VISITDY,  invar_join_l1=WORK.CE.VISITNUM, invar_join_r1=SDTM.TV.VISITNUM, inform_1=VISITDY)
map_definition:
Merges variables (<Source Dataset.Variable> to (<Target Dataset.Source Variable> where <Target Dataset.Join Variable>=<Source Dataset.Join Variable>

Example:
Merges variable TV.VISITDY to CE.VISITDY where TV.VISITNUM=CE.VISITNUM

description: Used in RELREC mappings to subset to one record only (domain relation)
map_definition: Subset to one record only (domain relation)

function_name: * Map Definition - All library names, dataset names, variable names and formats shall be in upper case. If there is a condition connected to the mapping the first row of Map Definition shall be ‘Subset the data using the logic: <Condition>’.
